body:
Hi Kenji,

Following up on my message from last week about early access at the Bellevue location. My standing routine is a 5 AM session before my shift days — I'm usually pulling into the parking structure by 4:50, and twice in the past month the front doors weren't unlocked until 5:08 or later. For most people that's a minor inconvenience. For me it compresses the whole session.

I've got a tight window: gym, matcha, breakfast, then school drop-off with my daughter before I head to the airport. If the door runs late, something gets cut — and it's never the flight. Yosh, I get that staffing has its own variables, but I wanted to flag it formally so it's on record.

Can you confirm whether the 5 AM open time is guaranteed or best-effort? And is there an escalation path if it happens again?

Thanks,
Daichi Mosley
Member #: BEL-04471

Hi Daichi,

Thank you for following up — and for framing it so clearly. You're right that 5:00 AM is the posted open time, and a consistent 8-minute delay isn't acceptable. I've flagged your two reported instances to the Bellevue club manager, and they're reviewing the door-unlock protocol with the opening staff this week.

Going forward, the front desk supervisor has your member number noted. If you arrive and the doors aren't open by 5:02, you can text the club's direct line (listed in your app under Location Info) and someone will open within two minutes. It's not a perfect fix, but it protects your window.

We'll also follow up with you directly once the manager confirms the staffing adjustment — should be within 48 hours. Appreciate your patience, and gambatte on the early sessions.

Best,
Kenji
Member Experience — Equinox Bellevue

Hi Kenji,

Quick follow-up on my order from last week — the ceremonial-grade Uji matcha, 100g tin. Tracking shows it's been sitting at the Bellevue distribution hub since Thursday with no movement. I'm out on a four-day rotation starting tomorrow morning, so if it doesn't land today it misses my window entirely.

The morning ritual is hard to overstate. Post-Equinox, before the house wakes up — that 25 minutes with a bowl and the day's notes is the steadiest part of my schedule. Without it, the day runs me instead of the other way around.

Can you check whether the package can be held for a signature waiver, or redirected to a pickup location? I'd rather collect it on return than gamble on the porch.

Daichi Mosley

Hi Daichi,

Thanks for reaching out. I pulled up your order — the delay is a sorting backlog at the Bellevue hub, but it's flagged for priority dispatch and estimated delivery is today before 5 PM.

I've added the signature waiver as requested. Alternatively, I can hold it at the nearest pickup facility, which is roughly 1.2 miles from your registered address. That option keeps it secure regardless of your departure time tomorrow.

Let me know which you'd prefer and I'll update the routing right away. Apologies for the slow tracking updates — I know that part's frustrating.

Best,
Kenji
Customer Support — Matcha Republic

Kenji,

Go with the hold at the pickup location. Cleaner option. I'll swing by Thursday afternoon when I'm back — okaeri to the tin, finally.

One thing worth passing to your team: the tracking portal went dark for 48 hours during the delay. That's the actual friction point. Not the delay itself — delays are part of the job, I get it — but flying without position updates is where patience runs out fast. If the portal surfaced even a static "carrier delay, no ETA" message, half these support contacts probably don't happen.

Appreciate you sorting it quickly.

Daichi

Hi Kenji,

Following up on the drop-off arrangement we discussed last week. My block-month schedule just posted — I'm on a four-day Bellevue-to-Phoenix rotation starting the 7th, wheels up at 0610. That wipes out my window for school drop-off Monday through Thursday.

Aisha can cover Monday and Tuesday no problem. Patricia has confirmed Wednesday, but I still need someone to verify Thursday with her directly — she wasn't sure about her own schedule when I checked in Sunday. Can you touch base with her and loop me in? Hana needs to be at the gate by 0820 or it creates problems with her classroom rotation.

Yosh, let's get this locked before the 5th.

Daichi

Hi Daichi,

Thanks for the heads-up on your rotation — got it noted. I'll reach out to Patricia today to confirm Thursday availability. Just to make sure I have the right contact: is this still the same number you provided in March, or did that change?

Also, quick clarification — if Patricia can't do Thursday, is Aisha the fallback, or is there another option you'd like me to flag first? Want to make sure I'm not burning a call to Aisha if she's already stretched those two days.

I'll have an answer back to you by end of day tomorrow at the latest.

Best,
Kenji
Family Support Coordination

Kenji,

Same number from March — that's still current. Patricia's reliable once she commits, it's just the confirming that takes a nudge.

On your fallback question: Aisha is the fallback, but check Patricia first. Aisha's mornings are already tight — she drops Kenji at preschool before circling back for Hana, so stacking Thursday on top would be a lot to ask. If Patricia falls through entirely, flag me before going to Aisha. I'll be in Bellevue Wednesday night so I can sort it if there's a problem.

Also — if you reach Patricia before noon she's much more responsive. After that it's hit or miss.

Gambatte.

Daichi

Hi Daichi,

All confirmed. Spoke with Patricia this afternoon — she's solid for Thursday. She mentioned she'd seen the 0820 gate note from before and already has it in her calendar, so no issue there.

I've updated the schedule in the system with Aisha covering Monday/Tuesday, Patricia on Wednesday and Thursday. If anything shifts on your rotation end, just send me the new block dates and I'll adjust accordingly.

Everything's set. Safe flying on the 7th.

Best,
Kenji
Family Support Coordination

Kenji,

Following up on last week's plan — are you still good to cover school pickup on Thursday? I've got a turn to SEA that's wheels-down around 1600, and even with an on-time block-in I won't make it to school by 1510. Patricia has Wednesday locked, but Thursday is the gap.

Pickup is 1510 at the front loop. Hana knows to wait by the flagpole if there's a line. Kenji Jr. usually drags his feet at dismissal, so give yourself an extra five minutes.

Text me before noon if anything changes — once I'm in pre-flight I'm heads-down until we're blocked in.

Yosh, appreciate you.
— Daichi

Daichi,

Thursday is confirmed. I'll be at the front loop by 1505, flagpole side. I know how you feel about a late pickup so I'll build in the buffer for your son's tactical slow walk.

One thing — Hana asked me on the phone last night whether she's coming to your taiko class Tuesday. She said she wants to watch. No idea if that's already a plan or she's lobbying, but figured you'd want the intel before she escalates to Aisha.

I'll text you once they're both buckled. Safe flight Thursday — gambatte.
— Kenji

Kenji,

Following up on the Gmail API integration we discussed last week. I finally had a window this morning after drop-off — got Hana to school, sat down with the matcha, and tried the OAuth flow again. Still hitting the same 403 on the `gmail.send` scope. Credentials are in place, consent screen is configured, but the token refresh is failing silently.

I've attached the error log from this morning's attempt. The timestamp on it is 0612 local — I was up early before my Equinox session and figured I'd get it out of the way. No luck.

Yosh. What am I missing here?

— Daichi

Daichi,

Thanks for the log — that helps a lot. The 403 on `gmail.send` is almost always a scope mismatch at token generation time. When you originally authorized, the send scope may not have been included in the initial consent request. The fix is straightforward: revoke the existing token at myaccount.google.com/permissions, then re-run the OAuth flow with the full scope string explicitly listed.

Also double-check your `redirect_uri` in the Cloud Console matches exactly what your code is sending — trailing slash differences will silently break the refresh without a clear error message.

Let me know if you hit a wall after revoking. I can pull up your project config on our end and verify the scope list matches what's registered.

— Kenji
Support Team

Kenji,

That was it. Revoked, re-authorized with the correct scope string, and it went through clean. First test send landed in the outbox without complaint. Took me maybe twelve minutes total — picked Kenji up from school, came back, ran it again, done.

One follow-up: is there a recommended way to handle token expiry gracefully in a script that runs on a schedule? Right now if the refresh token goes stale the whole thing fails quietly. I'd rather it surfaces an alert than dies silently — same philosophy I apply in the cockpit. Silent failures are the ones that get you.

Appreciate the fast turnaround on this.

— Daichi

Kenji,

Following up on the Google Calendar API ticket from last week — the OAuth refresh token issue is still happening. I'm running a script that pulls recurring weekly blocks into my personal calendar: 5 AM gym sessions, Tuesday evening taiko class, school drop-off and pickup windows. Worked fine for two months, then tokens stopped refreshing silently and everything fell out of sync.

I've verified the client credentials and confirmed the scope covers `calendar.events`. The failure pattern is specific to recurring event updates — one-off entries sync without issue. Happy to pull and share logs if that moves things faster.

— Daichi

Hi Daichi,

Thanks for the clear breakdown — that context helps a lot. Silent refresh token failure on recurring events is a known edge case, and the most common cause is a missing `access_type=offline` or `prompt=consent` flag during the initial OAuth flow. If `prompt=consent` wasn't included, Google may not have issued a new refresh token on subsequent authorizations, which would explain the silent failure.

Also worth checking: if your Cloud project's OAuth consent screen is still in Testing status, refresh tokens issued under it expire after 7 days. Publishing the app (or adding your account as a test user explicitly) resolves that.

Please share those log snippets when you get a chance and I'll dig in further.

— Kenji | Support

Kenji,

Yosh — that was it. `prompt=consent` was missing from the initial auth call. Re-ran the full flow with both `access_type=offline` and `prompt=consent` set, got a clean refresh token, and the recurring blocks are back in sync. Tuesday taiko and the school pickup windows are showing up correctly again.

One follow-up question while I have you: when I call `events.list` with a `timeMin`/`timeMax` window that overlaps the series start date, I'm getting what look like duplicate instances for some recurring events. Is that expected, or should I be passing `singleEvents=true` to flatten the response?

Appreciate the quick turnaround.

— Daichi

Hi Daichi,

Glad that resolved it — clean diagnosis on your end made it straightforward.

On the duplicates: yes, `singleEvents=true` is exactly what you want. Without it, `events.list` returns the recurring event as a single master object alongside any exception instances, which produces apparent duplicates when your query window overlaps the series origin. With `singleEvents=true`, the API expands each occurrence individually and `start.dateTime` is reliable for sorting and display.

One thing to watch: when `singleEvents=true` is set, switch your `orderBy` parameter to `startTime`. The `updated` sort order isn't supported in single-event mode and will return a 400 error. Should be a clean fix from there. Gambatte — let us know if anything else surfaces.

— Kenji | Support

Hi Kenji,

Following up on the Google Drive API issue from last week. The service account credentials I set up still aren't returning folder listings for the shared drives — getting a 403 on every call despite the domain-wide delegation being configured. I tested it Tuesday evening after taiko class, ran through the scopes again, nothing changed.

The specific scope I'm using is drive.readonly. I've also verified the client ID is added to the Admin Console. At this point I'm not sure if it's a propagation delay or something in the JSON key file itself.

Can you take a look at the delegation setup on your end? I'd rather get this sorted before my next block of days off — I'm juggling drop-off mornings and I don't want to be debugging OAuth flows between school runs.

Yosh, let's get this closed out.

Daichi

Hi Daichi,

Thanks for the follow-up — good timing, I had a chance to dig into the service account config this morning.

I found the issue: the client ID added to the Admin Console was the OAuth 2.0 client ID, not the service account's unique ID. They look similar but they're different values. You'll need to remove the current entry and re-add using the numeric ID from the service account's details page in Google Cloud Console — it's listed under "Unique ID," not "Client ID."

Once you swap that in, propagation typically takes 15–30 minutes. After that, the drive.readonly scope should work without the 403. If you're also planning to access shared drives specifically, add drive as a second scope — readonly alone can miss certain shared drive permissions depending on org settings.

Let me know how it goes after you make the swap. Happy to jump on a quick call if it's still throwing errors.

Kenji

Hi Kenji,

Following up on my ticket from last week regarding the WhatsApp Business API integration. I still haven't received the verification callback on the webhook endpoint I submitted, and I'm not sure if it's pending on your end or if something failed silently.

Schedule's been tight — layovers in and out of Bellevue all week — so I haven't had a clean window to troubleshoot from scratch. But I did confirm the endpoint is live and returning 200s. The phone number ID and access token I'm using match what's in the Meta dashboard exactly.

Can you check the status on your side? If there's a log of the verification attempt I can cross-reference, that would help me narrow it down fast.

Daichi Mosley

Hi Daichi,

Thanks for following up. I pulled the logs on our end and found the verification attempt did fire — on June 22nd at 14:03 UTC — but we received a 307 redirect response instead of the expected 200. That's what caused it to fail silently. The webhook verifier doesn't follow redirects by default.

Most common cause is an HTTPS redirect from HTTP, or a trailing-slash redirect on the URL. Worth double-checking the exact URL you submitted in the dashboard — even a small mismatch can trigger this. Once you've confirmed or updated the endpoint URL, I can manually re-trigger the verification attempt on our side so you don't have to re-register the number.

Let me know and I'll get it queued up promptly.

Kenji
WhatsApp Platform Support

Kenji,

Yosh — that was it. Trailing slash in the submitted URL that my server was redirecting away. Fixed the endpoint path and removed the redirect rule. Clean 200 now on a manual curl test.

Go ahead and re-trigger the verification when you get a chance. I'll keep an eye on it this evening after school pickup — should have a quiet window around 4:30 PM PT if anything needs a quick back-and-forth.

Appreciate you pulling the logs. That would have taken me considerably longer to find on my own.

Daichi Mosley

Hi Kenji,

Following up on the ticket I submitted last week regarding 5 AM early access at the Bellevue Equinox. I've built my mornings around that window — matcha and a light breakfast review after the session, then Hana's school drop-off — so the scheduling is pretty load-bearing. Problem is my membership swipe still isn't unlocking the door at 5:00. I'm standing in the parking lot until 5:28 when the standard tier kicks in.

If there's a tier upgrade required to get that access, let me know what it involves. My schedule runs in blocks and I'm only reliably home Tuesday through Thursday this rotation, so those morning slots are the ones I can actually protect.

Yosh — appreciate you looking into it.
Daichi Mosley

Hi Daichi,

Thanks for the follow-up and sorry for the friction on your end. I pulled your account and confirmed the issue — you're currently on Standard tier, which opens at 5:30 AM. The 5 AM early access is part of our All-Access Signature tier, so it requires an upgrade.

The difference is $35/month. I can process that today and have your access updated within the hour, or if you'd prefer I can flag this to our facilities team to see if there's a one-off accommodation available under your current plan. Either way, I want to get this resolved before your next session.

Just say the word and I'll move on it.

Best,
Kenji — Equinox Member Services

Kenji,

Process the upgrade. Thirty-five dollars a month is less than what I'd spend on a bad morning, and that 5 AM window is the only one that clears before drop-off. Hana's school start is fixed, so the back half of the morning doesn't flex.

One thing to confirm before you finalize: does All-Access Signature cover the Seattle downtown location as well? I'm sometimes near there on layover days. Useful to know, especially on Tuesdays — I have a taiko class in the evening and mornings are the only real training window I've got those days.

Thanks,
Daichi

Hi Daichi,

Done — upgrade is processed and your early access is live. All-Access Signature covers all three Seattle-area clubs, Bellevue and downtown included, so you're set on both ends.

Digital card has been pushed to your app. Tap at the main turnstile as usual. If the reader doesn't respond first morning, hard-refresh the app and it should sync within thirty seconds. If it's still not working after that, call the front desk and reference ticket #EQ-20847 — they'll get you in.

Gambatte on the Tuesday sessions.

Kenji — Equinox Member Services

Hi Kenji,

Following up on the breakfast routine question you had last week. The matcha ritual runs about 20 minutes — whisk, no shortcuts. I do a first pass of the day's brief or any crew notices while the water cools to temp. It's the one stretch of the morning that stays consistent regardless of whether I've got a 0600 report or a later push.

The quiet part matters. Aisha and the kids are usually still asleep, so I get that window before the house kicks into gear. Hana's been up earlier lately, so some days she sits across from me and draws. Doesn't break the rhythm — she's good at that.

Yosh, if there were follow-on questions from your side, send them over. Happy to walk through the specifics.

Daichi

Hi Daichi,

Thanks for the detail — that helps fill in the picture considerably. The 20-minute matcha block with the brief review makes a lot of sense as a transition ritual; low stimulation, high intention. The fact that it holds even on early report days says something about how embedded it is.

The note about Hana joining sometimes is a nice data point too. Routines that can absorb a small variable without breaking tend to be the sturdiest ones.

One follow-up: does the breakfast review stay on paper or are you pulling up a screen? Just want to understand the sensory profile of the window — light exposure, device use, that kind of thing. No rush on the response.

Kenji

Kenji,

Following up on last week's conversation about Hana and Kenji Jr.'s drop-off schedule. I've got an early sign-out this Thursday — wheels up at 0640, which means I'm out the door by 0445 after my Equinox session. Aisha has that endocrinology follow-up in the morning, so she can't cover it.

Can you check if Patricia is available to do the school run Thursday? Hana needs to be at Westridge by 8:15, Kenji Jr. at Lincoln no later than 8:30. If Patricia can't do both, Aisha might be back in time for Lincoln — just not Westridge.

Let me know what you find out. Yosh.

Daichi

Daichi,

Got it — I reached out to Patricia this morning. She's confirmed for Thursday. She can handle both stops: Westridge first, then Lincoln. She said she'll be at your place by 7:50 to give Hana enough time. I'll send her the parking notes for Westridge so she's not circling the lot again like last time.

Also flagged that Aisha's schedule has a conflict again the following Tuesday. You're on a Bellevue overnight that night, right? Want me to put Patricia on tentative hold for that morning too, or will Mom be in town by then?

Just say the word.

Kenji

Kenji,

Thursday is sorted — arigatou. Tell Patricia to text Aisha when she's five minutes out, just so the kids are ready at the door and she's not waiting.

On Tuesday — yes, I'm on the overnight. Mom gets in Monday evening, so she should be able to cover the morning. I'll confirm with her tonight after matcha. If something shifts I'll let you know by Sunday so Patricia has enough notice.

Appreciate you running this down. Okaeri vibes when I land Friday.

Daichi

Hi Kenji,

Following up on the pickup issue from last Thursday. I had an early Equinox session and a morning brief before departing with Ridgecrest — Aisha handled drop-off but wasn't available for the 3:15 window. Your team confirmed a driver would cover it, but Hana was standing outside for nearly 20 minutes before anyone arrived.

She's nine. That's not acceptable. I need to know where the gap was on the dispatch side and whether your confirmations actually reach drivers in real time.

Appreciate a quick turnaround on this.

Daichi Mosley

Hi Daichi,

Thank you for following up — I'm sorry Hana had that experience. I pulled the dispatch log for Thursday the 26th. The driver assigned to your route received the pickup notification at 3:08 PM but hit a route overlap with another school that caused a 17-minute delay. He should have sent a status update and did not — that's a gap on our end, not a system issue.

I've flagged this with our dispatch supervisor and updated your account to include a real-time SMS alert whenever a driver is running more than 5 minutes behind your scheduled window. You'll have notice before it becomes a wait.

Please let me know if anything else needs clarifying.

Kenji Mosley
Customer Support, BrightPath School Pickup

Kenji,

Yosh — SMS alert is the right fix, thank you. One follow-up: can the alert go to two numbers? Aisha should be on it as well. I fly Bellevue to Portland most weeks and there are days I'm airborne during the pickup window, so she's the first contact on those blocks.

Also — Tuesdays I leave around 6 PM for taiko, so if there's ever a late-day change on a Tuesday, earlier notice matters more than usual on that day.

Good response time on this.

Daichi

Hi Daichi,

Done — Aisha's number is now added to the alert profile alongside yours. Both of you will receive the 5-minute early warning SMS going forward. I've also added a priority note to your account flagging Tuesdays as a tighter-window day so dispatch has that context before scheduling.

Hope the rest of Hana's week runs without issue. Reach out anytime if something else comes up.

Best,
Kenji Mosley
Customer Support, BrightPath School Pickup
